note: The full Atrium protocol is deployed, wired, and verified on Robinhood Chain testnet (Arbitrum L2). The compute-heavy core (Plinth SPAN margin engine, Coffer ERC-4626 vault, Sigil EIP-712 mandates, Vigil liquidator) is Arbitrum Stylus (Rust) and was deployed + activated natively. Sigil is initialized; Coffer + Vigil have set_plinth wired; Plinth's constructor ran with all peer addresses. The Router resolves plinth/coffer/registry correctly and the Aave adapter reports version (1,1,0). Verified: Coffer.praetorMultisig == Plinth.praetorMultisig == deployer.

plinth.note: SPAN portfolio margin engine, Rust/Stylus. Constructor ran via the StylusDeployer.

coffer.note: ERC-4626 collateral vault, Rust/Stylus. set_plinth wired.

atrium-router.note: Orchestrates margin -> vault -> adapter. Wired to the live plinth/coffer/registry.

mock-usdc.note: Collateral asset (no canonical USDC on Robinhood Chain testnet); open mint.

stylus-deployer.note: Copy of the canonical Stylus deployer (the chain lacked one); required to deploy #[constructor] Stylus contracts.

remaining.note: Trade-fill wiring (Coffer.setAdapter(Router), adapter.setAuthorizedCaller(Router), registry.registerAdapter(venue, adapter)) is gated by the 48h PraetorTimelock, exactly as on Arbitrum Sepolia. Schedule + execute after 48h to enable a live fill; the contracts themselves are all deployed + wired + verified.
